questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is true?
questions.explanation: A trapezium needs at least one pair of parallel sides, which every parallelogram has. A kite has perpendicular diagonals but is not a rhombus.

answers.text: Every parallelogram is a trapezium (using the inclusive definition)
Every quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals is a rhombus

questions.question_text: What is the supplementary angle to 84°45'?
Using the inclusive definition of a trapezium, which of these quadrilaterals is NOT necessarily a trapezium?
questions.explanation: Rectangles, squares and parallelograms all have a pair of parallel sides; a kite need not.

questions.question_text: A quadrilateral has perpendicular diagonals but is not a rhombus. Which of these could it be?
questions.explanation: A (non-rhombic) kite has perpendicular diagonals; a rectangle's or general parallelogram's diagonals are not perpendicular, and a square is a rhombus.

answers.text: A parallelogram that is not a rhombus
